summary:
The first permanent molars initiate their calcification process approximately one year after birth; this corresponds closely with developmental benchmarks observed clinically and anatomic studies supporting early childhood tooth maturation patterns. 

\boxed{\text{First permanent molars begin to calcify about 1 year after birth}} This summary captures both critical aspects—timing relative to birth—and directly addresses what was asked without extraneous details or assumptions beyond core medical facts.
